topic: Eclipse magnetic ensemble (39 eclipses) — post-EXP-222 rewrite adversarial review
change_summary: EXP-222 applied: detail_verdict_text fully rewritten (1995 → 4787 chars, +2792) with explicit FSF-geometry retraction, Pearson r=0.9999 concession, ratio-vs-magnitude pivot, Section 1.8 cross-reference updated to 'The Coordinate Collapse', and 39-eclipse ensemble explicitly framed as retrospective Kim & Chang (2018) citation. detail_evidence paragraph 3 last sentence replaced (+544 chars) mirroring retraction and adding the ensemble-doesn't-use-FSF argument. tldr_verdict fully replaced with concession language. Propagates EXP-220 / WIN-010.c5 correction to WIN-068 per ISS-1398.
our_argument_summary: Chapman (1933) Sq mechanism + Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse ensemble explain the signal without any dome parameters. The dome's FSF = H(r)/r scaling is conceded as a genuine geometric derivation (Pearson r=0.9999), but FSF is a station-to-station ratio that multiplies the 2017 BOU observation for the separate 2026 forward projection and does not enter the 39-eclipse ensemble claim at all. The ensemble is thus a retrospective literature citation. Triple-count with WIN-010 and WIN-025 inflates the score for one mechanism.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (WIN-068 appears only as aggregate 'Version 50.6, 2026-03-12, 39 confirmed' — no per-WIN entry in raw-text; WIN text is written by the dome author into the ECM V51.x narrative, not surfaced in raw-text)
raw-text/03-predictions.txt (FSF table for 2026-08-12 eclipse: EBR FSF 1.029 through LER FSF 2.075 — this is the forward-projection table, NOT the 39-eclipse ensemble)
raw-text/04-coordinates.txt (V12 H(r) = 8537 x exp(-r/8619); r solved numerically from r*tan(polaris_elevation) = H(r))
raw-text/05-model.txt (V12 KEY DISCOVERY: H(r) IS NOT CONSTANT; H(r) = 8537 x exp(-r/8619))
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: For the 39-eclipse ensemble WIN-068 specifically: no calculation pipeline in the dome's code. The WIN cites Kim & Chang (2018) as confirming the ensemble signal. No dome-derived value is compared against the ensemble statistics. Separately, for the 2026 forward projection (WIN-010's prediction, reused here as cluster context), the dome computes per-station predicted delta_Z = -10.9 nT x coverage x FSF, where FSF = (H(r)/r)/(H(r_BOU)/r_BOU).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No validation logic exists for WIN-068 in monitor.py. The WIN appears in the count but the automation does not touch it. The 2026 forward projection table (nine stations) is hardcoded in 03-predictions.txt.
data_sources_used: Kim & Chang (2018) Advances in Space Research 61:2040-2054 — cited as ensemble data source
INTERMAGNET (referenced via Kim & Chang; no live fetch)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None applicable — WIN-068 is counted as confirmed based on Kim & Chang's publication, not on any dome predictive test with pass/fail tolerance.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None found. The rewrite correctly identifies monitor.py as having no WIN-068 logic (monitoring = 'none') and correctly identifies the forward-projection FSF table in 03-predictions.txt as separate from the ensemble citation. Independent verification of FSF = H(r)/r reproducing the nine-station table at Pearson r=0.9999 was performed in EXP-220 / WIN-010.c5 and is carried forward here.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no domain, no API call, no comparison logic, and no threshold for WIN-068. The 39-eclipse ensemble is a retrospective literature citation; there is no live fetch of INTERMAGNET 1991-2016 data and no re-computation against a dome-predicted value. The 2026 forward projection table lives only in 03-predictions.txt (text, not code).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Chapman (1933) / Yamazaki & Maute (2021) Sq conductivity framework predicts eclipse magnetic perturbations (Y increase, X/Z/F decrease, 30-50 deg peak, 180-min window) from first principles. Kim & Chang (2018) confirmed this across 39 eclipses. The dome relabels the same phenomenon as 'firmament-plate dielectric boundary shading' via 'pressurized toroidal cavity induction gradient' without changing any numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Kim & Chang published 2018; dome incorporated V51.0 early 2026 — eight-year gap. Chapman mechanism documented 1933 — 93-year gap. No independent pre-registration of an ensemble prediction by the dome author located.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The 39-eclipse ensemble claim itself has no dome-derived prediction attached — FSF only appears in the separate 2026 forward projection. For the ensemble, the dome cites Kim & Chang's published result without deriving any number from H(r), n(r), or any other dome parameter. (FSF = H(r)/r is conceded as genuine dome geometry for the forward projection, but that is WIN-010's prediction, not WIN-068's.)

holes_found.description: The finding field (summary-table text, rendered in the main WIN table) still asserts the retracted empirical-fitting claim verbatim: 'its FSF scaling formula is empirically calibrated per-station, not derived from disc-radius geometry'. This is the exact characterization that detail_verdict_text paragraph 2 and tldr_verdict now explicitly retract ('we retract our prior empirical fitting framing'; 'has been retracted in the WIN-010/025/068 cluster'). A dome defender grep'ing 'empirically calibrated' across the site will find the summary table contradicting the main text. Readers who skim the WIN table without clicking through see the retracted claim presented as our current position. EXP-222's scope was deliberately limited to detail_verdict_text / detail_evidence / tldr_verdict, but the finding field carries the same factual error and must be updated for the retraction to propagate cleanly. Note: WIN-010 has the parallel defect ('circularly calibrated' in its finding) — WIN-010.c6 did not flag this specifically; it should be addressed in the same pass.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the finding field. Suggested text: 'Non-discriminating: Chapman (1933) ionospheric mechanism + Kim & Chang (2018) predict the 39-eclipse ensemble signal without dome parameters. The 39-eclipse ensemble is a retrospective Kim & Chang citation; the dome's FSF = H(r)/r scaling (a genuine geometric derivation, Pearson r = 0.9999) is only invoked in the separate 2026 forward projection, where it multiplies the 2017 BOU observation rather than deriving absolute magnitudes from dome geometry. This WIN duplicates the observational basis of WIN-010 and WIN-025 at a larger aggregation level.' Open parallel issue for WIN-010 finding (uses 'circularly calibrated' which traces to the same retracted framing).

holes_found.description: The finding field phrase 'Dome adds no dome-derived equation' is now doubly problematic. (a) The dome DOES publish an equation (delta_Z = baseline x coverage x FSF) in raw-text/03-predictions.txt — flagged as moderate in c8 and not addressed. (b) Post-retraction, we now concede the FSF factor in that equation IS dome geometry (Pearson r = 0.9999). So the assertion 'no dome-derived equation' is not merely imprecise; it reads as the opposite of what we now acknowledge. A defender: 'You admit in paragraph 2 that the dome equation's scaling factor is genuine dome geometry, then in the summary you deny any dome-derived equation exists?' Elevated from moderate (c8) to major now that the retraction makes the contradiction explicit.
holes_found.recommendation: In the replacement finding (see previous hole), say 'the dome publishes a scaling equation but derives only the station-to-station ratio (FSF = H(r)/r), not the absolute magnitude at the Boulder anchor, which is imported from the 2017 observation' — concede the equation exists, concede the ratio derivation, keep the critique on absolute-magnitude derivation and ensemble retrospectivity. Sibling WINs (WIN-010/025) should be harmonized in the same pass.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence paragraph 3 uses 'by contrast' to introduce the concession that FSF genuinely equals H(r)/r at Pearson r=0.9999. Verbatim: 'The ionospheric parameters Sq models use ... are obtained from independent sources ... none of which are fitted to the eclipse magnetic signal being predicted. The dome's FSF scaling factor, by contrast, genuinely equals H(r)/r normalized to Boulder — direct recomputation reproduces the nine published FSF values at Pearson r = 0.9999, so the geometric derivation the dome claims is real and we retract our prior "empirical fitting" framing.' The rhetorical frame 'by contrast' normally sets up opposition, so a quote-mining defender can extract 'the dome's FSF scaling factor, by contrast, genuinely equals H(r)/r ... direct recomputation reproduces the nine published FSF values at Pearson r = 0.9999, so the geometric derivation the dome claims is real' as if our own review acknowledges the dome has a stronger derivation than standard physics. The argument that follows ('But FSF is a station-to-station ratio only...') does correct course, but a reader who stops at the Pearson-r sentence has a defender-friendly quote.
holes_found.recommendation: Recast as a concession-then-pivot rather than a contrast. Suggested: 'The dome's FSF scaling factor is a legitimate geometric derivation — direct recomputation confirms FSF = H(r)/r normalized to Boulder reproduces the nine published FSF values at Pearson r = 0.9999, and we retract our prior empirical-fitting framing. But that scaling is a ratio between stations only; it does not derive the absolute Boulder magnitude, which the 2026 forward projection imports from the 2017 observation. Standard Sq modeling, by contrast, derives absolute magnitudes at each station from ionospheric parameters sourced independently of the eclipse signal.' This inverts the rhetoric so 'by contrast' highlights the standard model's strength rather than the dome's.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN consistency: the analyst's EXP-222 cross_win_consistency_flag notes that WIN-025 retains the stale 'dome's refraction cascade' and 'sun's physical position seasonally indeterminate' language from before the 2026-04-12 Section 1.8 rename (HNOTE-REFRACT-RESTRUCTURE-001, Section 1.8 now titled 'The Coordinate Collapse'). WIN-068 post-EXP-222 explicitly references 'Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse)' with the n(r) 300-6000x refraction framing. WIN-025's current text still uses the older vocabulary. For the triple-count cluster argument ('WIN-010, WIN-025, and WIN-068 all invoke the same ionospheric Sq mechanism at three different aggregation levels') to land, a reader who clicks through all three panels should see consistent vocabulary and a consistent stance on the FSF retraction. WIN-025 does not assert FSF empirical fitting (so no factual error to retract), but its Section 1.8 cross-reference and supporting vocabulary are now inconsistent with WIN-010 c5/c6 and WIN-068 c9. I confirm the analyst's finding and recommend the decider open a low-priority EXP for WIN-025 vocabulary catch-up. Out of scope for EXP-222 per the package instructions; flagging to prevent it from slipping.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider: open issue 'WIN-025 Section 1.8 cross-reference + cluster-vocabulary catch-up'. Scope: replace 'dome's refraction cascade' and 'sun's physical position seasonally indeterminate' phrasing with 'Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse)' and the n(r) contamination framing used in WIN-010 c5/WIN-068 c9. Optionally add a one-sentence acknowledgment that the FSF = H(r)/r concession applies to WIN-025 as well (even though WIN-025's own text never asserted empirical fitting).

holes_found.description: detail_verdict_text paragraph 6 reads: 'Under the dome author's own timestamping definition, no pre-registration of the expected value in a venue independent of the author could be located.' The phrase 'dome author's own timestamping definition' is ambiguous — a reader could take it as (a) an acknowledgment that we accept his definition, or (b) a critique that even by his own standard he fails. We link to the Timestamp Error tab, which makes (b) the intended reading, but the sentence itself reads (a). Minor because the link disambiguates, but sharpening the phrasing removes a low-effort defender cavil.
holes_found.recommendation: Rephrase: 'Even by the dome author's own OpenTimestamps methodology (which we critique at #timestamp-error for anchoring observation records rather than predictions), no pre-registration of an ensemble prediction in a venue independent of the author could be located.' Or: 'The ensemble was not pre-registered in any independent venue — and even under the dome's own OpenTimestamps scheme, which #timestamp-error shows anchors only post-observation records, no prior claim exists.'

holes_found.description: detail_verdict_text paragraph 6 says 'no pre-registration of the expected value ... could be located.' The phrase 'the expected value' was imported from WIN-010, which has a specific magnitude (-10.9 nT) that could in principle be pre-registered. WIN-068 is an ensemble statistical finding across 39 eclipses — there is no single 'expected value' to pre-register. The claim for WIN-068 is 'the ensemble shows systematic Y/X/Z/F patterns at 30-50 deg' — a claim about a dataset, not a single value. The phrasing mismatches the nature of the WIN-068 claim.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'no pre-registration of the expected value' with 'no pre-registration of the ensemble claim' or 'no pre-registration of the Y/X/Z/F pattern or its latitude dependence in a venue independent of the author'. This fits the retrospective-citation framing and removes a defender opening ('which expected value?').

holes_found.description: detail_verdict_text paragraph 3 asserts: 'The scaling shape is also not uniquely dome-geometric: any smooth monotonic H(r) combined with r*tan(theta) = H(r) produces a latitude-dependent ratio of the same form.' This is stated without demonstration and was already flagged in WIN-010.c6 for the parallel text. Same defect here. Formally the claim is defensible — the r*tan(theta) = H(r) convention forces r to be a function of theta for any H, so FSF as a ratio in theta is H-function-dependent in numerical detail but latitude-dependent in every case — but a reader/defender sees an unargued assertion. Not critical because the ratio-is-weaker-than-magnitude argument doesn't rely on this claim, but if it stays in the text it should be demonstrated or rephrased.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add a worked example (e.g., 'substituting H(r) = 6000 x exp(-r/7000) into r*tan(theta) = H(r) produces a different numerical FSF at each station but preserves monotonic latitude dependence'), or (b) soften to 'the latitude-dependent shape is not a unique fingerprint of the exponential H(r) the dome uses; other smooth H(r) functions would yield similarly latitude-dependent ratios, so the 0.1% match identifies the dome's H curve but not the dome's underlying mechanism'.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence paragraph 2 remains the pre-rename 'refraction cascade-era' aside about the dome's own cavity mechanism ('the refraction fix leaves physical sun altitude indeterminate'). EXP-222 explicitly left this paragraph out of scope per tracker note and ISS-1398. The paragraph's self-inoculation ('applicable to the dome's own mechanism, not the Std Model Explains verdict above') still works formally, but post-rewrite the paragraph reads more disconnected from the rest of detail_evidence — which now uses the 'Coordinate Collapse / r contamination' framing. A future EXP should harmonize this paragraph with the new vocabulary. Recording for the decider's backlog; out of scope for this cycle.
holes_found.recommendation: Future EXP: harmonize detail_evidence P2 with the Section 1.8 'Coordinate Collapse' vocabulary used elsewhere in the WIN. Can be bundled with the WIN-025 cluster catch-up EXP (see cross-WIN consistency hole).

holes_found.description: Citation set carried forward without re-verification this cycle: (a) Chapman (1933) 'Terr. Mag. Atmos. Electr.' 38:3 — format still ambiguous (38:3 could mean Vol 38 Issue 3 or Vol 38 page 3; Chapman's 1931 ionospheric layer papers are in J. Roy. Astron. Soc. Canada); (b) Kim & Chang (2018) Adv. Space Res. 61:2040-2054 — cited as the 39-eclipse ensemble source; (c) Yamazaki & Maute (2021) DOI 10.3389/fspas.2021.766327 — cited for Sq conductivity framework with active hyperlink. All three remain uncheckable from the curmudgeon environment this cycle (no web access). Carried forward from c7 and c8 as citations_unchecked; logging here so successor reviews pick them up.
holes_found.recommendation: Future cycle (or analyst task): verify the three citations against the publisher pages. Particularly Chapman 1933 — the 'eclipse magnetic' attribution traces to Chapman 1931a 'The solar and lunar diurnal variations of terrestrial magnetism' (Phil. Trans. Royal Society London A, Vol 230). If our cited 'Chapman 1933' is actually Chapman 1931a or Chapman 1932 'The Lunar Diurnal Variation of Terrestrial Magnetism', fix the citation; the date shift does not harm the argument but is a defender-visible accuracy issue.

stronger_arguments.description: The rewrite's strongest new lever — 'the 39-eclipse ensemble counted here does not invoke FSF at all — it is a retrospective citation of Kim & Chang (2018) with no dome-derived prediction attached' — is more powerful than the parallel WIN-010 argument and should be foregrounded. WIN-010 has a specific observation (-10.9 nT at BOU 2017) that the dome weaves into its narrative. WIN-068 is PURELY a literature citation of a dataset from 1991-2016 that the dome had no involvement with. The 'no dome prediction here at all' argument is cleaner here. Currently this point is buried in paragraph 3 of detail_verdict_text. Moving it to the top — first sentence of verdict paragraph 1 — would strengthen the lede: 'The 39-eclipse ensemble counted as WIN-068 is a retrospective citation of Kim & Chang (2018, 1991-2016 data); no dome equation is derived or tested against it. The signal itself is the 90-year-old Chapman Sq mechanism, relabeled as firmament cavity induction.'
sources: raw-text/03-predictions.txt (confirms FSF table is for 2026-08-12, not 1991-2016)

stronger_arguments.description: Worth quantifying the 'ensemble coverage' critique: Kim & Chang (2018) is a statistical study of 39 specific eclipses, with geographic distribution and time range fixed by what INTERMAGNET recorded. The dome did not select which eclipses entered the study, did not specify the stations, and did not pre-register any expected ensemble statistic. This is the strongest form of 'score inflation via literature citation': a WIN credited for a dataset the dome had zero predictive contact with, across an 1991-2016 window that predates the entire ECM program. Adding a sentence to that effect would harden the post-hoc critique beyond the current generic 'eight-year gap' language.

kernel_of_truth.description: Three things the dome gets genuinely right here, all now correctly handled by the rewrite: (1) the 39-eclipse ensemble signal at the amplitudes Kim & Chang report is real peer-reviewed data; (2) FSF = (H(r)/r)/(H(r_BOU)/r_BOU) is a clean geometric derivation from the dome's V12 coordinate equation that reproduces the dome's published FSF values at Pearson r = 0.9999 — a legitimate derivation from dome parameters, as the rewrite now explicitly concedes and retracts our prior characterization; (3) the dome's Steelman Evidence Ladder is intellectually honest in placing eclipse geomagnetic evidence at Tier 1 (admitting the data is consistent with both models).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being right about three things does not add up to a dome-derived prediction of the 39-eclipse ensemble. The signal is predicted by Chapman 1933 + standard Sq modeling without any dome parameters. The FSF ratio derivation — real as it is — is invoked only for the separate 2026 forward projection, where it multiplies a 2017 observation rather than deriving absolute magnitudes from dome geometry. And the Steelman Tier 1 placement itself concedes the data does not discriminate. The rewrite correctly captures all three credits without letting them rescue the WIN-068 claim.

deeper_analysis: The EXP-222 rewrite is a clean propagation of the EXP-220 / WIN-010.c5 correction. The detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict retraction language is explicit, cites the Pearson r = 0.9999 verification, and correctly identifies the ensemble-does-not-use-FSF lever as WIN-068-specific (stronger than the parallel WIN-010 argument). The Section 1.8 cross-reference is updated to 'The Coordinate Collapse'. The cluster retraction note ('has been retracted in the WIN-010/025/068 cluster') appears in paragraph 2 and makes the cross-WIN consistency explicit for a reader who lands on WIN-068 first. Advocate-mode steelman: a dome defender reading the rewrite would find one quotable concession (the Pearson r = 0.9999 + 'the geometric derivation the dome claims is real' sentence) and one rhetorical misstep ('by contrast' introducing the concession). The defender's best counter is the summary-table finding contradiction — the retracted claim is still present verbatim in the field a casual reader sees first. Fixing the finding field, rewording the 'by contrast' introduction of the concession, and sharpening the two P6 minor phrasings would close the last defender-accessible holes in this WIN; the core verdict ('Std Model Explains' / non-discriminating) is sound. Cross-WIN consistency with WIN-025 is a real residual issue that should be tracked as a follow-up EXP rather than left to accumulate. Citation checks remain open as a known backlog item across the cluster. Recommended action: minor_edit — the structural argument is correct; the finding field and a few paragraph-level fixes would bring WIN-068 to parity with detail_verdict_text's new quality level.

citations_unchecked: Chapman (1933) 'Terr. Mag. Atmos. Electr.' 38:3 — volume/issue/page format ambiguous; may actually be Chapman 1931a (Phil. Trans. Royal Soc. London A Vol 230). Carried from c7, c8.
Kim & Chang (2018) Advances in Space Research 61:2040-2054 — cited as ensemble source; unverified.
Yamazaki & Maute (2021) DOI 10.3389/fspas.2021.766327 — Sq conductivity framework; active hyperlink in text; unverified.
